description: Fix off-by-one bug in `opentabs logs --lines N` that shows N-1 visible lines instead of N

userStories.title: Fix tailFile off-by-one when log file ends with a trailing newline
userStories.description: As a user, I want `opentabs logs --lines 5` to show exactly 5 lines so that the output matches what I requested
acceptanceCriteria: `opentabs logs --lines 5` shows exactly 5 non-empty lines of log output (not 4)
The fix does not break the `--plugin` filter behavior (which already works correctly because the empty trailing element is filtered out)
The fix does not break the `--follow` mode (follow offset based on fileSize is unaffected)
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/logs.ts, function `tailFile` (lines 42-71).

Root cause: Line 63 does `chunk.split('\n')` on the file content. When the log file ends with a trailing newline (which it always does — every log line ends with `\n`), this produces an empty string as the last array element. For example, `"line1\nline2\nline3\n".split('\n')` → `["line1", "line2", "line3", ""]`. Then line 69 does `lines.slice(-lineCount)` which counts the empty trailing element as one of the N lines, so the user only sees N-1 visible lines.

Fix: After splitting (line 63), filter out empty trailing elements before slicing. The simplest correct fix is to add a line after the split that removes a trailing empty string:

```typescript
let lines = chunk.split('\n');
if (lines.at(-1) === '') lines.pop();
```

Do NOT use `.filter(Boolean)` or `.filter(line => line !== '')` — that would remove intentionally empty lines in the middle of the output (which is unlikely for log files but semantically wrong). Only remove the trailing empty element caused by the file ending with `\n`.

The `--plugin` filter path (line 66-68) is not affected because `''.includes(filter)` returns false for any non-empty filter string, so the empty trailing element is already filtered out in that code path. The fix only affects the unfiltered path.

Verify with: create a log file ending with `\n`, call `tailFile` with lineCount=5, confirm exactly 5 lines are returned.
